subject: Ecology
title: Flightlessness
description: A fast breakdown of Flightlessness for builders.

hook.text: Islands are full of birds that forgot how to fly—because they didn't need to.

buildup.text: Without ground predators, flight becomes an expensive luxury that wastes energy.

discovery.text: Rails evolved flightlessness independently on dozens of Pacific islands.

twist.text: When humans brought cats and rats, flightless birds had no escape. Most went extinct.

climax.text: The dodo, the moa, and the elephant bird were all flightless island giants lost to human arrival.

punchline.text: Islands made them flightless. Humans made them extinct.

quiz.question: Why do many island birds evolve flightlessness?
options: No ground predators make flight unnecessary
There is nowhere to land after flying
